line 30: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n6. **Value-Specific**: `yaml: line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int`\n7. **Multi-Error**: Multiple errors in a single TypeError\n\n### YAML Type Tags Coverage\n\nAll **8 YAML type tags** are represented in error messages:\n\n| Tag | Type | Common Mismatches |\n|-----|------|-------------------|\n| `!!str` | string | Into int, bool, []string |\n| `!!int` | integer | Into string, float, bool |\n| `!!float` | float | Into int, string |\n| `!!bool` | boolean | Into string, int |\n| `!!seq` | sequence | Into string, map |\n| `!!map` | mapping | Into string, []string, struct |\n| `!!null` | null | Into string, int, bool |\n| `!!timestamp` | timestamp | Into string, int |\n\n### Common Type Mismatches\n\nThe most frequent error patterns:\n\n1. **String to Integer** (most common)\n   - `port: \"8080\"` → expects `int`\n   \n2. **String to Boolean** (very common)\n   - `enabled: \"true\"` → expects `bool`\n   \n3. **String to Array** (common)\n   - `hosts: \"localhost\"` → expects `[]string`\n   \n4. **Boolean to String** (common)\n   - `name: true` → expects `string`\n   \n5. **Map to Struct** (common)\n   - YAML object incompatible with struct fields\n\n## ARMOR-Specific Features\n\n### Enhanced Error Handling\n\nThe ARMOR codebase includes sophisticated TypeError handling:\n\n1. **Type Normalization**\n   - YAML tags → readable names (`!!str` → `string`)\n   - Go type variants → standard names (`int8`, `int16` → `integer`)\n   - Complex types → descriptive names (`[]string` → `array of string`)\n\n2. **Field {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_bea6ebb98a664968af5e250c","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# yaml.TypeError Message Formats Documentation\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThis document catalogs the various error message formats produced by `yaml.TypeError` in the ARMOR codebase, focusing on patterns found in the `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` library.\n6\t\n7\t## yaml.TypeError Structure\n8\t\n9\t```go\n10\t// From gopkg.in/yaml.v3\n11\ttype TypeError struct {\n12\t    Errors []string  // Slice of error strings describing type mismatches\n13\t}\n14\t```\n15\t\n16\tThe `yaml.TypeError` struct contains a slice of error strings, where each string represents a distinct type conversion failure.\n17\t\n18\t## Error Message Format Patterns\n19\t\n20\t### Pattern 1: Basic Line-Based Error\n21\t**Format**: `line <number>: cannot unmarshal <actual> into <expected>`\n22\t\n23\t**Examples**:\n24\t- `line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n25\t- `line 15: cannot unmarshal !!seq into []string`\n26\t- `line 20: cannot unmarshal !!map into struct`\n27\t\n28\t**Components**:\n29\t- `line <number>`: Line number where error occurred\n30\t- `cannot unmarshal`: Error indicator\n31\t- `<actual>`: Actual YAML type found (with YAML type tag like `!!str`)\n32\t- `into`: Separator\n33\t- `<expected>`: Expected Go type\n34\t\n35\t### Pattern 2: YAML-Prefixed Line Error\n36\t**Format**: `yaml: line <number>: cannot unmarshal <actual> into <expected>`\n37\t\n38\t**Examples**:\n39\t- `yaml: line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n40\t- `yaml: line 15: cannot unmarshal !!seq into []string`\n41\t\n42\t**Components**:\n43\t- `yaml:`: Prefix indicating YAML parsing context\n44\t- Rest same as Pattern 1\n45\t\n46\t### Pattern 3: Line and Column Error\n47\t**Format**: `line <number>, column <number>: cannot unmarshal <actual> into <expected>`\n48\t\n49\t**Examples**:\n50\t- `line 10, column 5: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n51\t\n52\t**Components**:\n53\t- `line <number>`: Line number\n54\t- `column <number>`: Column number within the line\n55\t- Rest same as Pattern 1\n56\t\n57\t### Pattern 4: Error Context Prefix\n58\t**Format**: `error at line <number>: cannot unmarshal <actual> into <expected>`\n59\t\n60\t**Examples**:\n61\t- `error at line 25: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n62\t\n63\t**Components**:\n64\t- `error at`: Context prefix\n65\t- `line <number>`: Line number\n66\t- Rest same as Pattern 1\n67\t\n68\t### Pattern 5: Nested Context Error\n69\t**Format**: `error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: line <number>: cannot unmarshal <actual> into <expected>`\n70\t\n71\t**Examples**:\n72\t- `error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: line 30: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n73\t\n74\t**Components**:\n75\t- `error converting YAML to JSON`: Nested context\n76\t- `yaml:`: YAML parsing context\n77\t- Rest same as Pattern 2\n78\t\n79\t### Pattern 6: Value-Specific Error\n80\t**Format**: `yaml: line <number>: cannot unmarshal !!str <value> into <expected>`\n81\t\n82\t**Examples**:\n83\t- `yaml: line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int`\n84\t\n85\t**Components**:\n86\t- Same as Pattern 2\n87\t- `<value>`: The actual value that caused the error (backtick-quoted)\n88\t\n89\t### Pattern 7: Multi-Error Format\n90\t**Format**: `yaml: unmarshal errors:\\n  <error1>\\n  <error2>...`\n91\t\n92\t**Examples**:\n93\t```yaml\n94\tyaml: unmarshal errors:\n95\t  line 5: cannot unmarshal !!seq into []string\n96\t  line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str into int\n97\t```\n98\t\n99\t**Components**:\n100\t- `yaml: unmarshal errors:`: Header\n101\t- Indented individual errors (one per line)\n102\t\n103\t## YAML Type Tags\n104\t\n105\tThe error messages use YAML type tags to indicate actual types:\n106\t\n107\t| Tag | Type | Description |\n108\t|-----|------|-------------|\n109\t| `!!str` | string | String/scalar text |\n110\t| `!!int` | integer | Integer number |\n111\t| `!!float` | float | Floating-point number |\n112\t| `!!bool` | boolean | Boolean value |\n113\t| `!!seq` | sequence | Array/sequence |\n114\t| `!!map` | mapping | Object/dictionary |\n115\t| `!!null` | null | Null/nil value |\n116\t| `!!timestamp` | timestamp | Date/time value |\n117\t\n118\t## Common Go Types in Errors\n119\t\n120\tExpected types in errors are Go type representations:\n121\t\n122\t### Basic Types\n123\t- `int`, `int8`, `int16`, `int32`, `int64`\n124\t- `uint`, `uint8`, `uint16`, `uint32`, `uint64`\n125\t- `float32`, `float64`\n126\t- `string`\n127\t- `bool`\n128\t\n129\t### Composite Types\n130\t- `[]string` - Array/slice of strings\n131\t- `[]int` - Array/slice of integers\n132\t- `map[string]interface{}` - Map with string keys\n133\t- `map[string]int` - Map with string keys to int values\n134\t- `struct` - Struct type\n135\t\n136\t### Pointer Types\n137\t- `*string` - Pointer to string\n138\t- `*int` - Pointer to integer\n139\t- `*CustomType` - Pointer to custom type\n140\t\n141\t### Interface Types\n142\t- `interface{}` - Empty interface (any type)\n143\t- `io.Reader` - Interface with specific methods\n144\t\n145\t## Error Message Analysis in ARMOR\n146\t\n147\t### Helper Functions in typeerror_helpers.go\n148\t\n149\tThe ARMOR codebase includes comprehensive parsing functions:\n150\t\n151\t1. **`ParseTypeErrorString`**: Main entry point for parsing error strings\n152\t2. **`extractLineFromTypeError`**: Extracts line numbers with multiple patterns\n153\t3. **`extractColumnFromTypeError`**: Extracts column information\n154\t4. **`extractTypeMismatchInfo`**: Parses field path and type information\n155\t5. **`normalizeYAMLType`**: Normalizes type tags to readable names\n156\t\n157\t### Pattern Matching Regex Patterns\n158\t\n159\tThe code uses sophisticated regex patterns to extract information:\n160\t\n161\t```go\n162\t// Line extraction patterns (in order of specificity)\n163\t`line\\s+(\\d+),\\s*column`      // \"line 10, column 5:\"\n164\t`yaml:\\s+line\\s+(\\d+):`       // \"yaml: line 15:\"\n165\t`line\\s+(\\d+):`                // \"line 10:\"\n166\t`at\\s+line\\s+(\\d+)`            // \"at line 25\"\n167\t`error\\s+at\\s+line\\s+(\\d+)`    // \"error at line 30\"\n168\t`^(\\d+):`                      // \"10:\" at start\n169\t```\n170\t\n171\t## Type Normalization\n172\t\n173\tARMOR normalizes type descriptions for consistency:\n174\t\n175\t### YAML Type Tag Conversions\n176\t- `!!str` → `string`\n177\t- `!!int` → `integer`\n178\t- `!!float` → `number`\n179\t- `!!bool` → `boolean`\n180\t- `!!seq` → `array`\n181\t- `!!map` → `object`\n182\t- `!!null` → `null`\n183\t\n184\t### Go Type Normalizations\n185\t- Array types: `[]string` → `array of string`\n186\t- Pointer types: `*string` → `pointer to string`\n187\t- Map types: `map[string]int` → `map[string]integer`\n188\t- Integer variants: `int8`, `int16`, `int32`, `int64` → `integer`\n189\t- Unsigned variants: `uint8`, `uint16`, `uint32`, `uint64` → `unsigned integer`\n190\t\n191\t## Field Path Patterns\n192\t\n193\tError messages may include field path information:\n194\t\n195\t### Simple Field Paths\n196\t- `field server.port type mismatch` → Field: `server.port`\n197\t- `field items[0].name cannot unmarshal` → Field: `items[0].name`\n198\t\n199\t### Array Indexing\n200\t- `items[0].name` - First item in array\n201\t- `servers[1].port` - Second item in array\n202\t- `data.values[2]` - Third item in nested array\n203\t\n204\t### Nested Paths\n205\t- `server.port` - Simple nested field\n206\t- `database.connection.timeout` - Multi-level nesting\n207\t- `metadata.annotations[\"key\"]` - Map-style access\n208\t\n209\t## Edge Cases and Special Patterns\n210\t\n211\t### 1. Quoted Values\n212\tWhen values are included in errors, they may be quoted:\n213\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int`\n214\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str \"world\" into bool`\n215\t\n216\t### 2. Type Inference\n217\tWhen actual types cannot be determined from tags, the code infers from values:\n218\t- `\"true\"` → boolean\n219\t- `\"123\"` → number  \n220\t- `\"text\"` → string\n221\t\n222\t### 3. Complex Go Types\n223\tErrors involving complex types:\n224\t- `cannot unmarshal !!seq into []map[string]interface{}`\n225\t- `cannot unmarshal !!map into struct { Name string; Age int }`\n226\t\n227\t### 4. Interface Types\n228\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str into interface{}`\n229\t- `cannot unmarshal !!map into io.Reader`\n230\t\n231\t## Related Files in ARMOR\n232\t\n233\t- `internal/yamlutil/errors.go` - Error type definitions\n234\t- `internal/yamlutil/typeerror_helpers.go` - TypeError parsing utilities\n235\t- `internal/yamlutil/validator.go` - YAML validation with TypeError handling\n236\t- `internal/yamlutil/yaml_typeerror_test.go` - Type assertion tests\n237\t- `internal/yamlutil/parse_type_error_test.go` - Error parsing tests\n238\t- `internal/yamlutil/typeerror_helpers_test.go` - Helper function tests\n239\t\n240\t## Summary\n241\t\n242\tThe `yaml.TypeError` in ARMOR produces structured error messages with these key characteristics:\n243\t\n244\t1. **Multiple format patterns** - 7+ distinct error message formats\n245\t2. **Line/column information** - Precise location data when available\n246\t3. **YAML type tags** - Standard YAML type indicators for actual values\n247\t4. **Go type names** - Expected types represented as Go type names\n248\t5. **Field paths** - Optional field path information for nested structures\n249\t6. **Multi-error support** - Can represent yaml.TypeError Message Formats Documentation\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document catalogs the various error message formats produced by `yaml.TypeError` in the ARMOR codebase, focusing on patterns found in the `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` library.\n\n## yaml.TypeError Structure\n\n```go\n// From gopkg.in/yaml.v3\ntype TypeError struct {\n    Errors []string  // Slice of error strings describing type mismatches\n}\n```\n\nThe `yaml.TypeError` struct contains a slice of error strings, where each string represents a distinct type conversion failure.\n\n## Error Message Format Patterns\n\n### Pattern 1: Basic Line-Based Error\n**Format**: `line <number>: cannot unmarshal <actual> into <expected>`\n\n**Examples**:\n- `line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n- `line 15: cannot unmarshal !!seq into []string`\n- `line 20: cannot unmarshal !!map into struct`\n\n**Components**:\n- `line <number>`: Line number where error occurred\n- `cannot unmarshal`: Error indicator\n- `<actual>`: Actual YAML type found (with YAML type tag like `!!str`)\n- `into`: Separator\n- `<expected>`: Expected Go type\n\n### Pattern 2: YAML-Prefixed Line Error\n**Format**: `yaml: line <number>: cannot unmarshal <actual> into <expected>`\n\n**Examples**:\n- `yaml: line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n- `yaml: line 15: cannot unmarshal !!seq into []string`\n\n**Components**:\n- `yaml:`: Prefix indicating YAML parsing context\n- Rest same as Pattern 1\n\n### Pattern 3: Line and Column Error\n**Format**: `line <number>, column <number>: cannot unmarshal <actual> into <expected>`\n\n**Examples**:\n- `line 10, column 5: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n\n**Components**:\n- `line <number>`: Line number\n- `column <number>`: Column number within the line\n- Rest same as Pattern 1\n\n### Pattern 4: Error Context Prefix\n**Format**: `error at line <number>: cannot unmarshal <actual> into <expected>`\n\n**Examples**:\n- `error at line 25: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n\n**Components**:\n- `error at`: Context prefix\n- `line <number>`: Line number\n- Rest same as Pattern 1\n\n### Pattern 5: Nested Context Error\n**Format**: `error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: line <number>: cannot unmarshal <actual> into <expected>`\n\n**Examples**:\n- `error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: line 30: cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n\n**Components**:\n- `error converting YAML to JSON`: Nested context\n- `yaml:`: YAML parsing context\n- Rest same as Pattern 2\n\n### Pattern 6: Value-Specific Error\n**Format**: `yaml: line <number>: cannot unmarshal !!str <value> into <expected>`\n\n**Examples**:\n- `yaml: line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int`\n\n**Components**:\n- Same as Pattern 2\n- `<value>`: The actual value that caused the error (backtick-quoted)\n\n### Pattern 7: Multi-Error Format\n**Format**: `yaml: unmarshal errors:\\n  <error1>\\n  <error2>...`\n\n**Examples**:\n```yaml\nyaml: unmarshal errors:\n  line 5: cannot unmarshal !!seq into []string\n  line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str into int\n```\n\n**Components**:\n- `yaml: unmarshal errors:`: Header\n- Indented individual errors (one per line)\n\n## YAML Type Tags\n\nThe error messages use YAML type tags to indicate actual types:\n\n| Tag | Type | Description |\n|-----|------|-------------|\n| `!!str` | string | String/scalar text |\n| `!!int` | integer | Integer number |\n| `!!float` | float | Floating-point number |\n| `!!bool` | boolean | Boolean value |\n| `!!seq` | sequence | Array/sequence |\n| `!!map` | mapping | Object/dictionary |\n| `!!null` | null | Null/nil value |\n| `!!timestamp` | timestamp | Date/time value |\n\n## Common Go Types in Errors\n\nExpected types in errors are Go type representations:\n\n### Basic Types\n- `int`, `int8`, `int16`, `int32`, `int64`\n- `uint`, `uint8`, `uint16`, `uint32`, `uint64`\n- `float32`, `float64`\n- `string`\n- `bool`\n\n### Composite Types\n- `[]string` - Array/slice of strings\n- `[]int` - Array/slice of integers\n- `map[string]interface{}` - Map with string keys\n- `map[string]int` - Map with string keys to int values\n- `struct` - Struct type\n\n### Pointer Types\n- `*string` - Pointer to string\n- `*int` - Pointer to integer\n- `*CustomType` - Pointer to custom type\n\n### Interface Types\n- `interface{}` - Empty interface (any type)\n- `io.Reader` - Interface with specific methods\n\n## Error Message Analysis in ARMOR\n\n### Helper Functions in typeerror_helpers.go\n\nThe ARMOR codebase includes comprehensive parsing functions:\n\n1. **`ParseTypeErrorString`**: Main entry point for parsing error strings\n2. **`extractLineFromTypeError`**: Extracts line numbers with multiple patterns\n3. **`extractColumnFromTypeError`**: Extracts column information\n4. **`extractTypeMismatchInfo`**: Parses field path and type information\n5. **`normalizeYAMLType`**: Normalizes type tags to readable names\n\n### Pattern Matching Regex Patterns\n\nThe code uses sophisticated regex patterns to extract information:\n\n```go\n// Line extraction patterns (in order of specificity)\n`line\\s+(\\d+),\\s*column`      // \"line 10, column 5:\"\n`yaml:\\s+line\\s+(\\d+):`       // \"yaml: line 15:\"\n`line\\s+(\\d+):`                // \"line 10:\"\n`at\\s+line\\s+(\\d+)`            // \"at line 25\"\n`error\\s+at\\s+line\\s+(\\d+)`    // \"error at line 30\"\n`^(\\d+):`                      // \"10:\" at start\n```\n\n## Type Normalization\n\nARMOR normalizes type descriptions for consistency:\n\n### YAML Type Tag Conversions\n- `!!str` → `string`\n- `!!int` → `integer`\n- `!!float` → `number`\n- `!!bool` → `boolean`\n- `!!seq` → `array`\n- `!!map` → `object`\n- `!!null` → `null`\n\n### Go Type Normalizations\n- Array types: `[]string` → `array of string`\n- Pointer types: `*string` → `pointer to string`\n- Map types: `map[string]int` → `map[string]integer`\n- Integer variants: `int8`, `int16`, `int32`, `int64` → `integer`\n- Unsigned variants: `uint8`, `uint16`, `uint32`, `uint64` → `unsigned integer`\n\n## Field Path Patterns\n\nError messages may include field path information:\n\n### Simple Field Paths\n- `field server.port type mismatch` → Field: `server.port`\n- `field items[0].name cannot unmarshal` → Field: `items[0].name`\n\n### Array Indexing\n- `items[0].name` - First item in array\n- `servers[1].port` - Second item in array\n- `data.values[2]` - Third item in nested array\n\n### Nested Paths\n- `server.port` - Simple nested field\n- `database.connection.timeout` - Multi-level nesting\n- `metadata.annotations[\"key\"]` - Map-style access\n\n## Edge Cases and Special Patterns\n\n### 1. Quoted Values\nWhen values are included in errors, they may be quoted:\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str \"world\" into bool`\n\n### 2. Type Inference\nWhen actual types cannot be determined from tags, the code infers from values:\n- `\"true\"` → boolean\n- `\"123\"` → number  \n- `\"text\"` → string\n\n### 3. Complex Go Types\nErrors involving complex types:\n- `cannot unmarshal !!seq into []map[string]interface{}`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!map into struct { Name string; Age int }`\n\n### 4. Interface Types\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str into interface{}`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!map into io.Reader`\n\n## Related Files in ARMOR\n\n- `internal/yamlutil/errors.go` - Error type definitions\n- `internal/yamlutil/typeerror_helpers.go` - TypeError parsing utilities\n- `internal/yamlutil/validator.go` - YAML validation with TypeError handling\n- `internal/yamlutil/yaml_typeerror_test.go` - Type assertion tests\n- `internal/yamlutil/parse_type_error_test.go` - Error parsing tests\n- `internal/yamlutil/typeerror_helpers_test.go` - Helper function tests\n\n## Summary\n\nThe `yaml.TypeError` in ARMOR produces structured error messages with these key characteristics:\n\n1. **Multiple format patterns** - 7+ distinct error message formats\n2. **Line/column information** - Precise location data when available\n3. **YAML type tags** - Standard YAML type indicators for actual values\n4. **Go type names** - Expected types represented as Go type names\n5. **Field paths** - Optional field path information for nested structures\n6. **Multi-error support** - Can represent multiple type errors in one TypeError\n\nThe ARMOR codebase includes comprehensive parsing and normalization of these error formats for enhanced debugging and error reporting.\n","numLines":252,"startLine":1,"totalLines":252}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_8bea0e97534c44348e5a3c4e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# yaml.TypeError Expected vs Actual Type Patterns\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThis document catalogs common expected vs actual type patterns found in `yaml.TypeError` messages in the ARMOR codebase. These patterns represent the most frequent type mismatches encountered during YAML unmarshaling.\n6\t\n7\t## Type Mismatch Categories\n8\t\n9\t### 1. String to Primitive Type Mismatches\n10\t\n11\t#### String to Integer\n12\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n13\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains `\"123\"` but target expects `int`\n14\t- **Common in**: Configuration files with numeric values\n15\t- **Examples**:\n16\t  - `port: \"8080\"` → expects `int`\n17\t  - `timeout: \"30\"` → expects `int`\n18\t  - `replicas: \"3\"` → expects `int`\n19\t\n20\t**Variants**:\n21\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str into int8`\n22\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str into int16`\n23\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str into int32`\n24\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str into int64`\n25\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str into uint`\n26\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str into uint8`\n27\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str into uint16`\n28\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str into uint32`\n29\t- `cannot unmarshal !!str into uint64`\n30\t\n31\t#### String to Boolean\n32\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into bool`\n33\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains `\"true\"` but target expects `bool`\n34\t- **Common in**: Feature flags and boolean configuration\n35\t- **Examples**:\n36\t  - `debug: \"true\"` → expects `bool`\n37\t  - `enabled: \"false\"` → expects `bool`\n38\t  - `verbose: \"yes\"` → expects `bool`\n39\t\n40\t#### String to Float\n41\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into float64`\n42\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains `\"3.14\"` but target expects `float64`\n43\t- **Common in**: Numeric configuration values\n44\t- **Examples**:\n45\t  - `rate: \"0.5\"` → expects `float64`\n46\t  - `ratio: \"1.5\"` → expects `float32`\n47\t  - `timeout: \"1.5\"` → expects `float64`\n48\t\n49\t### 2. Sequence/Array Type Mismatches\n50\t\n51\t#### String to Array\n52\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into []string`\n53\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains single string but target expects array\n54\t- **Common in**: List configuration fields\n55\t- **Examples**:\n56\t  - `hosts: \"localhost\"` → expects `[]string`\n57\t  - `tags: \"production\"` → expects `[]string`\n58\t  - `allowed_ips: \"192.168.1.1\"` → expects `[]string`\n59\t\n60\t#### Sequence to Wrong Array Type\n61\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into []int`\n62\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains array but wrong element type\n63\t- **Examples**:\n64\t  - `ports: [\"8080\", \"8081\"]` → expects `[]int`\n65\t  - `counts: [\"1\", \"2\", \"3\"]` → expects `[]int`\n66\t\n67\t#### Map to Array\n68\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!map into []string`\n69\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains object but target expects array\n70\t- **Examples**:\n71\t  - `servers: {host: localhost}` → expects `[]string`\n72\t\n73\t#### Array to Array Mismatch\n74\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into [][]string`\n75\t- **Scenario**: Nested array type mismatch\n76\t- **Examples**:\n77\t  - `matrix: [1, 2, 3]` → expects `[][]string`\n78\t\n79\t### 3. Map/Object Type Mismatches\n80\t\n81\t#### String to Map\n82\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into map[string]interface{}`\n83\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains string but target expects map\n84\t- **Examples**:\n85\t  - `metadata: \"value\"` → expects `map[string]interface{}`\n86\t\n87\t#### Sequence to Map\n88\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into map[string]string`\n89\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains array but target expects map\n90\t- **Examples**:\n91\t  - `labels: [key1, key2]` → expects `map[string]string`\n92\t\n93\t#### Integer to Map\n94\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!int into map[string]int`\n95\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains number but target expects map\n96\t- **Examples**:\n97\t  - `counts: 5` → expects `map[string]int`\n98\t\n99\t### 4. Boolean Type Mismatches\n100\t\n101\t#### Boolean to String\n102\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!bool into string`\n103\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains boolean but target expects string\n104\t- **Examples**:\n105\t  - `name: true` → expects `string`\n106\t  - `description: false` → expects `string`\n107\t\n108\t#### Boolean to Integer\n109\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!bool into int`\n110\t- **Scenario**: YAML contains boolean but target expects integer\n111\t- **Examples**:\n112\t  - `count: true` → expects `int`\n113\t  - `port: false` → expects `int`\n114\t\n115\t#### String to Boolean (Reversed)\n116\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into bool`\n117\t- **Scenario**: String representation of boolean\n118\t- **Examples**:\n119\t  - `enabled: \"true\"` → expects `bool`\n120\t  - `active: \"yes\"` → expects `bool`\n121\t\n122\t### 5. Numeric Type Mismatches\n123\t\n124\t#### Integer to Float\n125\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!int into float64`\n126\t- **Scenario**: Integer provided but float expected\n127\t- **Examples**:\n128\t  - `rate: 5` → expects `float64`\n129\t  - `ratio: 3` → expects `float32`\n130\t\n131\t#### Float to Integer\n132\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!float into int`\n133\t- **Scenario**: Float provided but integer expected\n134\t- **Examples**:\n135\t  - `count: 3.5` → expects `int`\n136\t  - `port: 8080.0` → expects `int`\n137\t\n138\t#### String to Float\n139\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into float64`\n140\t- **Scenario**: String number but float expected\n141\t- **Examples**:\n142\t  - `rate: \"0.5\"` → expects `float64`\n143\t  - `price: \"99.99\"` → expects `float64`\n144\t\n145\t### 6. Null Type Mismatches\n146\t\n147\t#### Null to String\n148\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!null into string`\n149\t- **Scenario**: Null value but string expected\n150\t- **Examples**:\n151\t  - `name: null` → expects `string`\n152\t\n153\t#### Null to Primitive\n154\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!null into int`\n155\t- **Scenario**: Null value but primitive expected\n156\t- **Examples**:\n157\t  - `count: null` → expects `int`\n158\t  - `enabled: null` → expects `bool`\n159\t\n160\t### 7. Struct Type Mismatches\n161\t\n162\t#### Map to Struct\n163\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!map into struct`\n164\t- **Scenario**: YAML object incompatible with struct fields\n165\t- **Examples**:\n166\t  - YAML: `{name: \"test\", value: \"data\"}`\n167\t  - Struct: `{Name string; Count int}`\n168\t\n169\t#### String to Struct\n170\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into struct`\n171\t- **Scenario**: String value but struct expected\n172\t- **Examples**:\n173\t  - `config: \"value\"` → expects `struct`\n174\t\n175\t### 8. Pointer Type Mismatches\n176\t\n177\t#### String to Pointer\n178\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into *string`\n179\t- **Scenario**: String value but pointer to string expected\n180\t- **Examples**:\n181\t  - `name: \"test\"` → expects `*string`\n182\t\n183\t#### Value to Pointer\n184\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!int into *int`\n185\t- **Scenario**: Non-pointer value but pointer expected\n186\t- **Examples**:\n187\t  - `count: 5` → expects `*int`\n188\t\n189\t### 9. Interface Type Mismatches\n190\t\n191\t#### Wrong Type to Interface\n192\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into interface{}`\n193\t- **Scenario**: Type incompatible with interface constraint\n194\t- **Examples**:\n195\t  - Type constraint requires specific type but string provided\n196\t\n197\t#### Map to Wrong Interface\n198\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!map into io.Reader`\n199\t- **Scenario**: Map provided but specific interface expected\n200\t- **Examples**:\n201\t  - `reader: {key: value}` → expects `io.Reader`\n202\t\n203\t### 10. Complex Type Mismatches\n204\t\n205\t#### Nested Array Mismatches\n206\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into [][]string`\n207\t- **Scenario**: Flat array but nested array expected\n208\t- **Examples**:\n209\t  - `matrix: [1, 2, 3]` → expects `[][]string`\n210\t\n211\t#### Map Value Type Mismatch\n212\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into map[string]int`\n213\t- **Scenario**: Array provided but map with specific value type expected\n214\t- **Examples**:\n215\t  - `counts: [1, 2, 3]` → expects `map[string]int`\n216\t\n217\t#### Array Size Mismatch\n218\t**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into [5]int`\n219\t- **Scenario**: Variable-length array but fixed-size expected\n220\t- **Examples**:\n221\t  - `values: [1, 2]` → expects `[5]int`\n222\t\n223\t## Common Scenarios\n224\t\n225\t### Configuration File Errors\n226\t\n227\t1. **Port Configuration**\n228\t   ```yaml\n229\t   # ERROR: String instead of integer\n230\t   server:\n231\t     port: \"8080\"  # should be: 8080\n232\t   ```\n233\t   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n234\t\n235\t2. **Boolean Flags**\n236\t   ```yaml\n237\t   # ERROR: String instead of boolean\n238\t   features:\n239\t     enabled: \"true\"  # should be: true\n240\t   ```\n241\t   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into bool`\n242\t\n243\t3. **Array Fields**\n244\t   ```yaml\n245\t   # ERROR: String instead of array\n246\t   hosts: \"localhost\"  # should be: [\"localhost\"]\n247\t   ```\n248\t   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into []string`\n249\t\n250\t### Data Structure Errors\n251\t\n252\t1. **Nested Configuration**\n253\t   ```yaml\n254\t   # ERROR: Type mismatch in nested field\n255\t   database:\n256\t     timeout: \"30\"  # should be: 30\n257\t   ```\n258\t   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n259\t\n260\t2. **Map vs Array**\n261\t   ```yaml\n262\t   # ERROR: Array instead of map\n263\t   labels: [key1, key2]  # should be: {key1: value1, key2: value2}\n264\t   ```\n265\t   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into map[string]string`\n266\t\n267\t3. **Null Values**\n268\t   ```yaml\n269\t   # ERROR: Null instead of required value\n270\t   name: null  # should be: \"value\"\n271\t   ```\n272\t   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!null into string`\n273\t\n274\t## Type Compatibility Matrix\n275\t\n276\t| Actual \\ Expected | string | int | bool | []string | map | struct |\n277\t|-------------------|--------|-----|------|----------|-----|---------|\n278\t| !!str | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |\n279\t| !!int | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |\n280\t| !!float | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |\n281\t| !!bool | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |\n282\t| !!seq | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |\n283\t| !!map | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |\n284\t| !!null | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |\n285\t\n286\tLegend:\n287\t- ✓ = Compatible (no error)\n288\t- ✗ = Incompatible (produces TypeError)\n289\t\n290\t## Error Detection and Resolution\n291\t\n292\t### Detection Patterns\n293\t\n294\t1. **Line Number Extraction**\n295\t   - Pattern: `line (\\d+)`\n296\t   - Used to locate the problematic line in YAML files\n297\t\n298\t2. **Type Tag Identification**\n299\t   - Pattern: `!!(\\w+)`\n300\t   - Identifies the actual YAML type encountered\n301\t\n302\t3. **Expected Type Extraction**\n303\t   - Pattern: `into (\\S+)`\n304\t   - Identifies the target Go type\n305\t\n306\t4. **Value Extraction**\n307\t   - Pattern: `` `([^`]+)` ``\n308\t   - Extracts the actual value when provided\n309\t\n310\t### Resolution Strategies\n311\t\n312\t1. **For String to Primitive Errors**\n313\t   - Remove quotes from numeric values\n314\t   - Convert `true`/`false` to unquoted booleans\n315\t   - Ensure numeric values don't contain extra characters\n316\t\n317\t2. **For Array/Sequence Errors**\n318\t   - Convert single values to arrays: `value` → `[value]`\n319\t   - Ensure array elements match expected types\n320\t   - Check for proper YAML array syntax\n321\t\n322\t3. **For Map/Object Errors**\n323\t   - Verify map syntax with proper key-value pairs\n324\t   - Ensure keys are strings\n325\t   - Check value types match expected map value types\n326\t\n327\t4. **For Null Value Errors**\n328\t   - Replace `null` with appropriate default values\n329\t   - Remove `null` for required fields\n330\t   - Use empty values (`\"\"`, `[]`, `{}`) where appropriate\n331\t\n332\t## ARMOR-Specific Patterns\n333\t\n334\t### Type Normalization\n335\t\n336\tARMOR includes sophisticated type normalization:\n337\t\n338\t```go\n339\t// YAML Type Tag Normalizations\n340\t\"!!str\"   → \"string\"\n341\t\"!!int\"   → \"integer\"\n342\t\"!!float\" → \"number\"\n343\t\"!!bool\"  → \"boolean\"\n344\t\"!!seq\"   → \"array\"\n345\t\"!!map\"   → \"object\"\n346\t\"!!null\"  → \"null\"\n347\t```\n348\t\n349\t```go\n350\t// Go Type Normalizations\n351\t\"int8\", \"int16\", \"int32\", \"int64\" → \"integer\"\n352\t\"uint8\", \"uint16\", \"uint32\", \"uint64\" → \"unsigned integer\"\n353\t\"float32\", \"float64\" → \"number\"\n354\t\"[]string\" → \"array of string\"\n355\t\"*string\" → \"pointer to string\"\n356\t```\n357\t\n358\t### Enhanced Error Messages\n359\t\n360\tARMOR enhances TypeError messages with:\n361\t\n362\t1. **Field Path Information**\n363\t   - `server.port type mismatch`\n364\t   - `items[0].name cannot unmarshal`\n365\t\n366\t2. **Line and Column Details**\n367\t   - `line 10, column 5: cannot unmarshal`\n368\t   - Precise location information\n369\t\n370\t3. **Contextual Information**\n371\t   - `value: 'actual_value'`\n372\t   - `expected: int, got: string`\n373\t\n374\t4. **Human-Readable Types**\n375\t   - `expected integer, got string`\n376\t   - `expected array of string, got string`\n377\t\n378\t## Testing and Validation\n379\t\n380\t### Test Coverage\n381\t\n382\tThe ARMOR codebase includes comprehensive test coverage:\n383\t\n384\t1. **Format Pattern Tests** (`parse_type_error_test.go`)\n385\t   - Tests all 7+ error message formats\n386\t   - Validates line/column extraction\n387\t   - Confirms type tag parsing\n388\t\n389\t2. **Helper Function Tests** yaml.TypeError Expected vs Actual Type Patterns\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document catalogs common expected vs actual type patterns found in `yaml.TypeError` messages in the ARMOR codebase. These patterns represent the most frequent type mismatches encountered during YAML unmarshaling.\n\n## Type Mismatch Categories\n\n### 1. String to Primitive Type Mismatches\n\n#### String to Integer\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains `\"123\"` but target expects `int`\n- **Common in**: Configuration files with numeric values\n- **Examples**:\n  - `port: \"8080\"` → expects `int`\n  - `timeout: \"30\"` → expects `int`\n  - `replicas: \"3\"` → expects `int`\n\n**Variants**:\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str into int8`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str into int16`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str into int32`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str into int64`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str into uint`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str into uint8`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str into uint16`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str into uint32`\n- `cannot unmarshal !!str into uint64`\n\n#### String to Boolean\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into bool`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains `\"true\"` but target expects `bool`\n- **Common in**: Feature flags and boolean configuration\n- **Examples**:\n  - `debug: \"true\"` → expects `bool`\n  - `enabled: \"false\"` → expects `bool`\n  - `verbose: \"yes\"` → expects `bool`\n\n#### String to Float\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into float64`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains `\"3.14\"` but target expects `float64`\n- **Common in**: Numeric configuration values\n- **Examples**:\n  - `rate: \"0.5\"` → expects `float64`\n  - `ratio: \"1.5\"` → expects `float32`\n  - `timeout: \"1.5\"` → expects `float64`\n\n### 2. Sequence/Array Type Mismatches\n\n#### String to Array\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into []string`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains single string but target expects array\n- **Common in**: List configuration fields\n- **Examples**:\n  - `hosts: \"localhost\"` → expects `[]string`\n  - `tags: \"production\"` → expects `[]string`\n  - `allowed_ips: \"192.168.1.1\"` → expects `[]string`\n\n#### Sequence to Wrong Array Type\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into []int`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains array but wrong element type\n- **Examples**:\n  - `ports: [\"8080\", \"8081\"]` → expects `[]int`\n  - `counts: [\"1\", \"2\", \"3\"]` → expects `[]int`\n\n#### Map to Array\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!map into []string`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains object but target expects array\n- **Examples**:\n  - `servers: {host: localhost}` → expects `[]string`\n\n#### Array to Array Mismatch\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into [][]string`\n- **Scenario**: Nested array type mismatch\n- **Examples**:\n  - `matrix: [1, 2, 3]` → expects `[][]string`\n\n### 3. Map/Object Type Mismatches\n\n#### String to Map\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into map[string]interface{}`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains string but target expects map\n- **Examples**:\n  - `metadata: \"value\"` → expects `map[string]interface{}`\n\n#### Sequence to Map\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into map[string]string`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains array but target expects map\n- **Examples**:\n  - `labels: [key1, key2]` → expects `map[string]string`\n\n#### Integer to Map\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!int into map[string]int`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains number but target expects map\n- **Examples**:\n  - `counts: 5` → expects `map[string]int`\n\n### 4. Boolean Type Mismatches\n\n#### Boolean to String\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!bool into string`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains boolean but target expects string\n- **Examples**:\n  - `name: true` → expects `string`\n  - `description: false` → expects `string`\n\n#### Boolean to Integer\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!bool into int`\n- **Scenario**: YAML contains boolean but target expects integer\n- **Examples**:\n  - `count: true` → expects `int`\n  - `port: false` → expects `int`\n\n#### String to Boolean (Reversed)\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into bool`\n- **Scenario**: String representation of boolean\n- **Examples**:\n  - `enabled: \"true\"` → expects `bool`\n  - `active: \"yes\"` → expects `bool`\n\n### 5. Numeric Type Mismatches\n\n#### Integer to Float\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!int into float64`\n- **Scenario**: Integer provided but float expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `rate: 5` → expects `float64`\n  - `ratio: 3` → expects `float32`\n\n#### Float to Integer\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!float into int`\n- **Scenario**: Float provided but integer expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `count: 3.5` → expects `int`\n  - `port: 8080.0` → expects `int`\n\n#### String to Float\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into float64`\n- **Scenario**: String number but float expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `rate: \"0.5\"` → expects `float64`\n  - `price: \"99.99\"` → expects `float64`\n\n### 6. Null Type Mismatches\n\n#### Null to String\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!null into string`\n- **Scenario**: Null value but string expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `name: null` → expects `string`\n\n#### Null to Primitive\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!null into int`\n- **Scenario**: Null value but primitive expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `count: null` → expects `int`\n  - `enabled: null` → expects `bool`\n\n### 7. Struct Type Mismatches\n\n#### Map to Struct\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!map into struct`\n- **Scenario**: YAML object incompatible with struct fields\n- **Examples**:\n  - YAML: `{name: \"test\", value: \"data\"}`\n  - Struct: `{Name string; Count int}`\n\n#### String to Struct\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into struct`\n- **Scenario**: String value but struct expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `config: \"value\"` → expects `struct`\n\n### 8. Pointer Type Mismatches\n\n#### String to Pointer\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into *string`\n- **Scenario**: String value but pointer to string expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `name: \"test\"` → expects `*string`\n\n#### Value to Pointer\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!int into *int`\n- **Scenario**: Non-pointer value but pointer expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `count: 5` → expects `*int`\n\n### 9. Interface Type Mismatches\n\n#### Wrong Type to Interface\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into interface{}`\n- **Scenario**: Type incompatible with interface constraint\n- **Examples**:\n  - Type constraint requires specific type but string provided\n\n#### Map to Wrong Interface\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!map into io.Reader`\n- **Scenario**: Map provided but specific interface expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `reader: {key: value}` → expects `io.Reader`\n\n### 10. Complex Type Mismatches\n\n#### Nested Array Mismatches\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into [][]string`\n- **Scenario**: Flat array but nested array expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `matrix: [1, 2, 3]` → expects `[][]string`\n\n#### Map Value Type Mismatch\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into map[string]int`\n- **Scenario**: Array provided but map with specific value type expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `counts: [1, 2, 3]` → expects `map[string]int`\n\n#### Array Size Mismatch\n**Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into [5]int`\n- **Scenario**: Variable-length array but fixed-size expected\n- **Examples**:\n  - `values: [1, 2]` → expects `[5]int`\n\n## Common Scenarios\n\n### Configuration File Errors\n\n1. **Port Configuration**\n   ```yaml\n   # ERROR: String instead of integer\n   server:\n     port: \"8080\"  # should be: 8080\n   ```\n   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n\n2. **Boolean Flags**\n   ```yaml\n   # ERROR: String instead of boolean\n   features:\n     enabled: \"true\"  # should be: true\n   ```\n   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into bool`\n\n3. **Array Fields**\n   ```yaml\n   # ERROR: String instead of array\n   hosts: \"localhost\"  # should be: [\"localhost\"]\n   ```\n   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into []string`\n\n### Data Structure Errors\n\n1. **Nested Configuration**\n   ```yaml\n   # ERROR: Type mismatch in nested field\n   database:\n     timeout: \"30\"  # should be: 30\n   ```\n   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!str into int`\n\n2. **Map vs Array**\n   ```yaml\n   # ERROR: Array instead of map\n   labels: [key1, key2]  # should be: {key1: value1, key2: value2}\n   ```\n   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!seq into map[string]string`\n\n3. **Null Values**\n   ```yaml\n   # ERROR: Null instead of required value\n   name: null  # should be: \"value\"\n   ```\n   **Error**: `cannot unmarshal !!null into string`\n\n## Type Compatibility Matrix\n\n| Actual \\ Expected | string | int | bool | []string | map | struct |\n|-------------------|--------|-----|------|----------|-----|---------|\n| !!str | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |\n| !!int | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |\n| !!float | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |\n| !!bool | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |\n| !!seq | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |\n| !!map | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |\n| !!null | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |\n\nLegend:\n- ✓ = Compatible (no error)\n- ✗ = Incompatible (produces TypeError)\n\n## Error Detection and Resolution\n\n### Detection Patterns\n\n1. **Line Number Extraction**\n   - Pattern: `line (\\d+)`\n   - Used to locate the problematic line in YAML files\n\n2. **Type Tag Identification**\n   - Pattern: `!!(\\w+)`\n   - Identifies the actual YAML type encountered\n\n3. **Expected Type Extraction**\n   - Pattern: `into (\\S+)`\n   - Identifies the target Go type\n\n4. **Value Extraction**\n   - Pattern: `` `([^`]+)` ``\n   - Extracts the actual value when provided\n\n### Resolution Strategies\n\n1. **For String to Primitive Errors**\n   - Remove quotes from numeric values\n   - Convert `true`/`false` to unquoted booleans\n   - Ensure numeric values don't contain extra characters\n\n2. **For Array/Sequence Errors**\n   - Convert single values to arrays: `value` → `[value]`\n   - Ensure array elements match expected types\n   - Check for proper YAML array syntax\n\n3. **For Map/Object Errors**\n   - Verify map syntax with proper key-value pairs\n   - Ensure keys are strings\n   - Check value types match expected map value types\n\n4. **For Null Value Errors**\n   - Replace `null` with appropriate default values\n   - Remove `null` for required fields\n   - Use empty values (`\"\"`, `[]`, `{}`) where appropriate\n\n## ARMOR-Specific Patterns\n\n### Type Normalization\n\nARMOR includes sophisticated type normalization:\n\n```go\n// YAML Type Tag Normalizations\n\"!!str\"   → \"string\"\n\"!!int\"   → \"integer\"\n\"!!float\" → \"number\"\n\"!!bool\"  → \"boolean\"\n\"!!seq\"   → \"array\"\n\"!!map\"   → \"object\"\n\"!!null\"  → \"null\"\n```\n\n```go\n// Go Type Normalizations\n\"int8\", \"int16\", \"int32\", \"int64\" → \"integer\"\n\"uint8\", \"uint16\", \"uint32\", \"uint64\" → \"unsigned integer\"\n\"float32\", \"float64\" → \"number\"\n\"[]string\" → \"array of string\"\n\"*string\" → \"pointer to string\"\n```\n\n### Enhanced Error Messages\n\nARMOR enhances TypeError messages with:\n\n1. **Field Path Information**\n   - `server.port type mismatch`\n   - `items[0].name cannot unmarshal`\n\n2. **Line and Column Details**\n   - `line 10, column 5: cannot unmarshal`\n   - Precise location information\n\n3. **Contextual Information**\n   - `value: 'actual_value'`\n   - `expected: int, got: string`\n\n4. **Human-Readable Types**\n   - `expected integer, got string`\n   - `expected array of string, got string`\n\n## Testing and Validation\n\n### Test Coverage\n\nThe ARMOR codebase includes comprehensive test coverage:\n\n1. **Format Pattern Tests** (`parse_type_error_test.go`)\n   - Tests all 7+ error message formats\n   - Validates line/column extraction\n   - Confirms type tag parsing\n\n2. **Helper Function Tests** (`typeerror_helpers_test.go`)\n   - Tests type normalization\n   - Validates field path extraction\n   - Confirms error message formatting\n\n3. **Integration Tests** (`yaml_typeerror_test.go`)\n   - Tests type assertions across codebase\n   - Validates error information preservation\n   - Confirms integration with parsers/validators\n\n### Fixture Usage\n\nThe test fixtures (`typeerror_test_fixtures.go`) provide:\n\n1. **Format-Specific Fixtures**\n   - Grouped by error message pattern\n   - Cover all identified formats\n\n2. **Type-Specific Fixtures**\n   - Grouped by YAML type tag\n   - Grouped by target Go type\n\n3. **Real-World Scenarios**\n   - Common configuration errors\n   - Typical data structure issues\n\n## Summary\n\nThe `yaml.TypeError` expected vs actual type patterns in ARMOR demonstrate:\n\n1. **High Frequency Categories**\n   - String to primitive types (most common)\n   - Array/sequence mismatches (very common)\n   - Boolean vs string confusion (common)\n\n2. **Complex Scenarios**\n   - Nested type mismatches\n   - Pointer type handling\n   - Interface type constraints\n\n3. **Comprehensive Coverage**\n   - All YAML type tags covered\n   - All common Go types covered\n   - Real-world scenarios represented\n\nThe ARMOR codebase includes robust parsing, normalization, and error handling for these patterns, making it easier to diagnose and resolve type mismatches in YAML configuration files.\n","numLines":435,"startLine":1,"totalLines":435}}}
